content: We can hypothesize that combining the multi-perspective reflection design from LLM self-reflection mechanisms with the unsupervised iterative framework of Self-Refine will resolve the core limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: it often gets stuck in local optimization loops because it only evaluates output from a single fixed perspective. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision, while adding structured perspective diversity to break out of repetitive unimproving refinement cycles.
